purpose: Estimate the probability of a large (20%+) G10 FX move given current rate divergence conditions, replacing prior ad-hoc estimates with formal Bayesian updating
components: Beta-Binomial updating with ~6 hits in ~10 qualifying episodes
Sensitivity analysis across skeptical (35%), baseline (40%), and informative (45%) priors
Episode identification criteria for qualifying historical events
current_assessment: Posterior of 35-50% replaces original 61% estimate. Remarkably robust to prior choice — data-driven. However, wide CI constrains use for precise position sizing. Episode identification problem (n=7 vs n=15) is the primary residual uncertainty, shifting posterior by ±5pp.
limitations: 95% CI [21%, 61%] too wide for precise position sizing
Episode identification problem shifts posterior by ±5pp
Reference distribution non-stationarity may further reduce signal from extreme to moderate
Does not incorporate regime-specific adjustments for post-2008 structural changes
